title: PLG vs sales-led motion decision
description: Your B2B SaaS has both self-serve signups and an outbound sales team. Decide where to invest: double down on PLG, shift to sales-led, or run both.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at FlowAtlas, a Series B B2B SaaS company (240 employees, $26.9M ARR). Your immediate assignment is PLG vs sales-led motion decision. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Treat credibility with customers as a hard constraint, not a soft preference.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 92109
New logo adds: 3700
Activation rate: 44%
30-day retention: 52%
NPS: 34 (n=144)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 27%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 1 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: -4 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Nora Cole): "Buyers are asking for proof points, not intent. We need visible traction fast."
VP Engineering (Owen Foster): "Scope drift, not coding speed, is the main reason timelines slip. Lock boundaries now."
Head of Customer Success (Noah Cole): "Trust loss shows up before churn in expansion conversations."
Commercial note: 5 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 26 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Mariner Foods (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Frontier Labs (IT Manager): "Peak-hour reliability issues are why leadership hesitates to expand usage."
Helio Health Group (Director of Product): "The concept is right, but edge-case handling still feels unfinished."
Data gap: no week-two cohort notes for downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 8
Median cycle time: 28 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 55%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 8
Release rollback events last quarter: 3
Average time to resolve escalations: 29 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: State your decision in one sentence, then justify it with evidence and constraints.
submissionFields.label: Decision and Recommendation

submissionFields.hint: Compare at least two viable alternatives and what each sacrifices.
submissionFields.label: Alternatives and Trade-offs

submissionFields.hint: Define the primary segment and who is explicitly deprioritized.
submissionFields.label: Who This Optimizes For

submissionFields.hint: List top risks and concrete guardrails to limit downside.
submissionFields.label: Key Risks and Guardrails

submissionFields.hint: Break down phases, owners, and stop/go checkpoints.
submissionFields.label: 90-Day Execution Plan

rubric.name: Decision Quality
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is the recommendation specific, evidence-based, and timely?

rubric.name: Trade-off Rigor
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are trade-offs explicit, quantified where possible, and defensible?

rubric.name: Stakeholder Awareness
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does the proposal handle competing incentives credibly?

rubric.name: Intellectual Honesty
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are uncertainty and assumptions clearly acknowledged?

expertSolution.content: Recommendation: run a bounded pilot with reliability guardrails. Baseline today is activation 44% and 30-day retention 52% across 92109 active accounts. Scale only if activation improves by at least 4 points without retention regression.

Option A is broad rollout now to support near-term sales urgency tied to 5 late-stage deals. Option B is reliability-only focus with deferred expansion. Option C combines targeted rollout with dependency reduction from 8 unresolved items. Option C is the best risk-adjusted path.

Optimize for repeat-use mid-market teams where onboarding friction is most visible in recent escalations (26). Deprioritize bespoke edge cases this cycle because cycle time is 28 days and on-time delivery is 55%.

Key risks are dependency slippage, overreliance on projected revenue, and trust erosion from reliability events. Guardrails: weekly dependency review with Owen Foster, explicit non-goals, and rollback if escalation volume increases more than 15% week-over-week.

Weeks 1-2 lock scope and instrumentation. Weeks 3-6 run pilot and reliability fixes. Weeks 7-10 evaluate cohort movement against activation/retention deltas and NPS confidence limits (n=144). Weeks 11-12 decide scale, pivot, or stop with documented trade-offs.
